title: Envisioning the next 150 years of crop research with the Alma Mater Plots
excerpt: A couple of miles south of the main quad at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, a remarkable agricultural experiment is taking shape. The so-called Alma Mater Plots are unique in their setup—with 64 plots of 1 acre (0.4 hectares) each, individually tile-drained, it is the largest experiment of its kind anywhere. To maximize the experiment's value to Illinois and Midwest agriculture, scientists are teaming up with farmers to decide what, exactly, to study.
content_html:
A couple of miles south of the main quad at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, a remarkable agricultural experiment is taking shape. The so-called Alma Mater Plots are unique in their setup—with 64 plots of 1 acre (0.4 hectares) each, individually tile-drained, it is the largest experiment of its kind anywhere. To maximize the experiment's value to Illinois and Midwest agriculture, scientists are teaming up with farmers to decide what, exactly, to study.

"I was trained in an international ag development context in eastern Africa and South America, where we'd roll up to a region and spend a week or more acclimating. During that time, we'd just shut up and listen to understand what people perceive as problems. I thought, we do this a lot in international ag development, but why not here?

"In my opinion, you just do better science if you listen to the people who work the land," said Andrew Margenot, director of the Morrow Plots and Alma Mater Plots and a professor in the Department of Crop Sciences in the College of Agricultural, Consumer and Environmental Sciences at U. of I.

Margenot's philosophy explains why the plots have been laid out for two years but long-term experimental treatments have not yet been finalized.

The research team has spent the past year assembling a diverse advisory board and seeking input directly from Illinois agriculturalists. Ping-ponging across the state, they have conducted more than a dozen focus groups to date—attended by more than 140 farmers—and they are still going.

Margenot plans to gather feedback from farmers, as well as industry and policy stakeholders, through the end of 2026.

Sanjay Sofat, environmental policy director for the Illinois Farm Bureau, is on the advisory board for the Alma Mater Plots. He appreciates Margenot's thorough approach to gathering information from farmers.

"There's a huge disconnect around the realities of farming. People make comments like, 'Hey, farmers should do XYZ,' without really understanding what it entails to adopt new practices," he said. "Farmers have to worry about and assess risk around financing new equipment, weather constraints, and so many other issues. By designing this experiment based on farmers' input, we hope farmers will feel more comfortable adopting practices that benefit the land and their bottom lines."

Morrow Plots 2.0

The Alma Mater Plots are designed to carry the tradition of the historic Morrow Plots forward for future generations. Celebrating their 150th year in 2026, U. of I.'s Morrow Plots are the second-oldest continuously running agricultural experiment in the world.

"The whole point of the Morrow Plots was to address questions that Illinois farmers had, as part of our land-grant mission," said ACES Dean Germán Bollero. "That's how it really began. They invited farmers to campus for extension days at the Morrow Plots. It was a big deal for the farm community. We're trying to bring that back for the next 150 years."

The current iteration of the Morrow Plots—a single acre (0.4 hectares) with 24 subplots—can fit inside one of the 64 Alma Mater subplots. With enough land set aside to eventually expand to 75 or more plots, the Alma Mater Plots represent a more realistic scale and afford researchers much greater statistical power to make confident management recommendations.

Emerging themes

While the researchers are still gathering farmers' opinions about what to test, several themes are emerging, including water quality, soil health, regenerative practices and profitability.

A key feature of the Alma Mater Plots is their individually monitored drainage tiles, which allow researchers to test the effects of cropping systems, inputs and management practices on water quality in real time. Since the plots were established in 2024, the researchers have been collecting baseline data on water quality.

"If we are going to farm some of the wettest soils in the country, we need tile. But we are very passive about tile—we tend to put them in and walk away. Farmers could be managing water a lot more intentionally," Margenot said. "Are there trade-offs to tile drainage? Absolutely. Let's optimize those trade-offs, in particular increasing nitrogen fertilizer use efficiency and profitability, and minimizing nitrate losses."

Margenot also wants to design the experiment in a way that helps farmers grow more profitably. For example, he wants to test whether they can avoid spending on herbicides and fertilizers by growing legume cover crops.

Reducing herbicide use is on Jude Holscher's mind. Farming in Crawford County with his dad, Holscher is a member of the Illinois Farm Bureau and his county's Soil and Water Conservation District. He learned about one of Margenot's focus groups and figured, "Why not?"

"They didn't have any decisions made on what they wanted to do with the research plots, so they were just getting insight from different farmers in the area," he said. "I didn't hold back on what I thought, and said I wanted to see more regenerative concepts in a U. of I. research plot."

Regenerative practices just make sense to Holscher as he looks to the future.

"My boys are 2 and 4. They really changed my perspective on a lot of things that we're doing. If you're on a tractor, they want to be with you. But they don't get to ride in the [herbicide] sprayer because of what you're surrounded with. I told my 4-year-old I'm hoping that by the time he's old enough to run this sprayer, we won't have to use it anymore. That's really a long-term goal."

Beth Baranski volunteers as the organizing secretary of the farmer-led Jo Daviess County Soil and Water Health Coalition. She attended a focus group on behalf of her organization, whose members are also increasingly interested in regenerative practices.

"Transitioning from conventional to regenerative practices presents challenges and can be a daunting move for farmers. We have a slowly growing set of early adopters in Jo Daviess County, but believe the practices can help farmers broadly," she said. "We think it's important that regenerative practices be represented in the plots to allow researchers to understand and compare the benefits so they can share this with the many farmers who rely on the university for guidance."

The next 150 years

The plots haven't lain fallow while the researchers have been gathering information. All are planted in corn for a 3-year Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research study to test-drive a proposed protocol for using new enhanced-efficiency fertilizers.

But when all the focus group feedback has been synthesized and the first long-term experimental treatments are implemented, Margenot says there will still be aspects not set in stone.

"We're designing the treatments such that the specifics can be modified—as the Morrow Plots have been innumerable times—to reflect the reality of the ever-changing agricultural landscape," he said. "We don't know what the questions will be in 50 years, much less 150, but we're planning for the Alma Mater Plots to keep helping farmers answer those questions for at least that long."

Who's behind this story?

Lisa Lock

BA art history, MA material culture. Former museum editor, paramedic, and transplant coordinator. Editing for Science X since 2021. Full profile →

Robert Egan

Bachelor's in mathematical biology, Master's in creative writing. Well-traveled with unique perspectives on science and language. Full profile →

Citation: Envisioning the next 150 years of crop research with the Alma Mater Plots (2026, July 20) retrieved 21 July 2026 from https://phys.org/news/2026-07-envisioning-years-crop-alma-mater.html

content_text: Researchers at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign are working with farmers and stakeholders to design a new long-term agricultural experiment that will deliver practical, evidence-based recommendations. A key feature of the experiment, known as the Alma Mater Plots, is that each of the 64+ acre-sized plots are individually tile drained to monitor water quality. Credit: Jim Baltz, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign A couple of miles south of the main quad at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, a remarkable agricultural experiment is taking shape. The so-called Alma Mater Plots are unique in their setup—with 64 plots of 1 acre (0.4 hectares) each, individually tile-drained, it is the largest experiment of its kind anywhere. To maximize the experiment's value to Illinois and Midwest agriculture, scientists are teaming up with farmers to decide what, exactly, to study. "I was trained in an international ag development context in eastern Africa and South America, where we'd roll up to a region and spend a week or more acclimating. During that time, we'd just shut up and listen to understand what people perceive as problems. I thought, we do this a lot in international ag development, but why not here? People make comments like, 'Hey, farmers should do XYZ,' without really understanding what it entails to adopt new practices," he said. "Farmers have to worry about and assess risk around financing new equipment, weather constraints, and so many other issues. Let's optimize those trade-offs, in particular increasing nitrogen fertilizer use efficiency and profitability, and minimizing nitrate losses."

sentences.en: "If we are going to farm some of the wettest soils in the country, we need tile.
sentences.zh: “如果我们要耕种该国一些最潮湿的土壤，我们需要瓷砖。

sentences.en: But we are very passive about tile—we tend to put them in and walk away.
sentences.zh: 但我们对瓷砖非常被动——我们倾向于把它们放进去然后走开。

sentences.en: Farmers could be managing water a lot more intentionally," Margenot said.
sentences.zh: 农民可以更有意识地管理水资源，”马格诺特说。

sentences.en: "Are there trade-offs to tile drainage?
sentences.zh: “瓷砖排水是否需要权衡？

sentences.en: Absolutely.
sentences.zh: 绝对地。

sentences.en: Let's optimize those trade-offs, in particular increasing nitrogen fertilizer use efficiency and profitability, and minimizing nitrate losses."
sentences.zh: 让我们优化这些权衡，特别是提高氮肥的使用效率和盈利能力，并最大限度地减少硝酸盐损失。”

blocks.html_en: Margenot also wants to design the experiment in a way that helps farmers grow more profitably. For example, he wants to test whether they can avoid spending on herbicides and fertilizers by growing legume cover crops.

sentences.en: Margenot also wants to design the experiment in a way that helps farmers grow more profitably.
sentences.zh: 马吉诺还希望以帮助农民种植更有利可图的方式设计实验。

sentences.en: For example, he wants to test whether they can avoid spending on herbicides and fertilizers by growing legume cover crops.
sentences.zh: 例如，他想测试他们是否可以通过种植豆类覆盖作物来避免除草剂和化肥方面的支出。

blocks.html_en: Reducing herbicide use is on Jude Holscher's mind. Farming in Crawford County with his dad, Holscher is a member of the Illinois Farm Bureau and his county's Soil and Water Conservation District. He learned about one of Margenot's focus groups and figured, "Why not?"

sentences.en: Reducing herbicide use is on Jude Holscher's mind.
sentences.zh: 裘德·霍尔舍 (Jude Holscher) 正在考虑减少除草剂的使用。

sentences.en: Farming in Crawford County with his dad, Holscher is a member of the Illinois Farm Bureau and his county's Soil and Water Conservation District.
sentences.zh: 霍尔舍尔与父亲一起在克劳福德县务农，他是伊利诺伊州农业局和该县水土保持区的成员。

sentences.en: He learned about one of Margenot's focus groups and figured, "Why not?"
sentences.zh: 他了解了玛吉诺特的一个焦点小组后想：“为什么不呢？”

blocks.html_en: "They didn't have any decisions made on what they wanted to do with the research plots, so they were just getting insight from different farmers in the area," he said. "I didn't hold back on what I thought, and said I wanted to see more regenerative concepts in a U. of I. research plot."

sentences.en: "They didn't have any decisions made on what they wanted to do with the research plots, so they were just getting insight from different farmers in the area," he said.
sentences.zh: “他们没有就研究地块的用途做出任何决定，所以他们只是从该地区不同农民那里获得见解，”他说。

sentences.en: "I didn't hold back on what I thought, and said I wanted to see more regenerative concepts in a U.
sentences.zh: “我没有隐瞒自己的想法，并表示我希望在美国看到更多再生概念。

sentences.en: of I.
sentences.zh: 我的。

sentences.en: research plot."
sentences.zh: 研究情节。”

blocks.html_en: Regenerative practices just make sense to Holscher as he looks to the future.

sentences.en: Regenerative practices just make sense to Holscher as he looks to the future.
sentences.zh: 当霍尔舍尔展望未来时，再生实践对他来说很有意义。

blocks.html_en: "My boys are 2 and 4. They really changed my perspective on a lot of things that we're doing. If you're on a tractor, they want to be with you. But they don't get to ride in the [herbicide] sprayer because of what you're surrounded with. I told my 4-year-old I'm hoping that by the time he's old enough to run this sprayer, we won't have to use it anymore. That's really a long-term goal."

sentences.en: "My boys are 2 and 4.
sentences.zh: “我的两个儿子分别是 2 岁和 4 岁。

sentences.en: They really changed my perspective on a lot of things that we're doing.
sentences.zh: 他们确实改变了我对我们正在做的很多事情的看法。

sentences.en: If you're on a tractor, they want to be with you.
sentences.zh: 如果你在拖拉机上，他们想和你在一起。

sentences.en: But they don't get to ride in the [herbicide] sprayer because of what you're surrounded with.
sentences.zh: 但由于你周围的环境，他们无法乘坐[除草剂]喷雾机。

sentences.en: I told my 4-year-old I'm hoping that by the time he's old enough to run this sprayer, we won't have to use it anymore.
sentences.zh: 我告诉我 4 岁的孩子，我希望当他长大到可以使用这台喷雾器时，我们就不再需要使用它了。

sentences.en: That's really a long-term goal."
sentences.zh: 这确实是一个长期目标。”

blocks.html_en: Beth Baranski volunteers as the organizing secretary of the farmer-led Jo Daviess County Soil and Water Health Coalition. She attended a focus group on behalf of her organization, whose members are also increasingly interested in regenerative practices.

sentences.en: Beth Baranski volunteers as the organizing secretary of the farmer-led Jo Daviess County Soil and Water Health Coalition.
sentences.zh: 贝丝·巴兰斯基 (Beth Baranski) 自愿担任农民领导的乔戴维斯县土壤和水健康联盟的组织秘书。

sentences.en: She attended a focus group on behalf of her organization, whose members are also increasingly interested in regenerative practices.
sentences.zh: 她代表她的组织参加了一个焦点小组，该小组的成员对再生实践也越来越感兴趣。

blocks.html_en: "Transitioning from conventional to regenerative practices presents challenges and can be a daunting move for farmers. We have a slowly growing set of early adopters in Jo Daviess County, but believe the practices can help farmers broadly," she said. "We think it's important that regenerative practices be represented in the plots to allow researchers to understand and compare the benefits so they can share this with the many farmers who rely on the university for guidance."

sentences.en: "Transitioning from conventional to regenerative practices presents challenges and can be a daunting move for farmers.
sentences.zh: “从传统做法向再生做法的转变带来了挑战，对农民来说可能是一个艰巨的举动。

sentences.en: We have a slowly growing set of early adopters in Jo Daviess County, but believe the practices can help farmers broadly," she said.
sentences.zh: 我们在乔戴维斯县有一批缓慢增长的早期采用者，但相信这些做法可以广泛帮助农民，”她说。

sentences.en: "We think it's important that regenerative practices be represented in the plots to allow researchers to understand and compare the benefits so they can share this with the many farmers who rely on the university for guidance."
sentences.zh: “我们认为，重要的是在地块中体现再生实践，以便研究人员能够理解和比较其好处，以便他们可以与许多依赖大学指导的农民分享这一点。”

blocks.html_en: The next 150 years

sentences.en: The next 150 years
sentences.zh: 未来150年

blocks.html_en: The plots haven't lain fallow while the researchers have been gathering information. All are planted in corn for a 3-year Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research study to test-drive a proposed protocol for using new enhanced-efficiency fertilizers.

sentences.en: The plots haven't lain fallow while the researchers have been gathering information.
sentences.zh: 当研究人员一直在收集信息时，这些地块并没有闲置。

sentences.en: All are planted in corn for a 3-year Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research study to test-drive a proposed protocol for using new enhanced-efficiency fertilizers.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都被种植在玉米中，用于食品和农业研究基金会为期三年的研究，以测试使用新型高效肥料的拟议方案。

blocks.html_en: But when all the focus group feedback has been synthesized and the first long-term experimental treatments are implemented, Margenot says there will still be aspects not set in stone.

sentences.en: But when all the focus group feedback has been synthesized and the first long-term experimental treatments are implemented, Margenot says there will still be aspects not set in stone.
sentences.zh: 但是，当所有焦点小组的反馈都得到综合并实施第一个长期实验治疗时，马吉诺特说，仍然会有一些方面不是一成不变的。

blocks.html_en: "We're designing the treatments such that the specifics can be modified—as the Morrow Plots have been innumerable times—to reflect the reality of the ever-changing agricultural landscape," he said. "We don't know what the questions will be in 50 years, much less 150, but we're planning for the Alma Mater Plots to keep helping farmers answer those questions for at least that long."

sentences.en: "We're designing the treatments such that the specifics can be modified—as the Morrow Plots have been innumerable times—to reflect the reality of the ever-changing agricultural landscape," he said.
sentences.zh: 他说：“我们正在设计处理方法，以便可以修改具体细节，因为莫罗地块已经出现过无数次了，以反映不断变化的农业景观的现实。”

sentences.en: "We don't know what the questions will be in 50 years, much less 150, but we're planning for the Alma Mater Plots to keep helping farmers answer those questions for at least that long."
sentences.zh: “我们不知道 50 年后会出现什么问题，更不用说 150 年后了，但我们正在计划母校地块至少在那么长的时间内继续帮助农民回答这些问题。”

sentences.zh: 引文：利用母校图展望未来 150 年的作物研究（2026 年，7 月 20 日），2026 年 7 月 21 日检索自 https://phys.org/news/2026-07-envisioning-years-crop-alma-mater.html

content_html_zh:
在伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校主校区以南几英里处，一项非凡的农业实验正在形成。 所谓的母校地块在其设置上是独一无二的——有 64 块地块，每块面积 1 英亩（0.4 公顷），单独铺设瓷砖排水，这是同类实验中规模最大的。 为了最大限度地发挥该实验对伊利诺伊州和中西部农业的价值，科学家们正在与农民合作，决定到底要研究什么。

“我在东非和南美洲的国际农业发展环境中接受了培训，我们将在那里聚集到一个地区，并花一周或更长时间来适应。 在那段时间里，我们只是闭嘴并倾听以了解人们认为的问题。 我想，我们在国际农业发展中经常这样做，但为什么在这里不呢？

莫罗地块和母校地块主任、美国大学农业、消费者和环境科学学院作物科学系教授安德鲁·马格诺特 (Andrew Margenot) 说：“在我看来，如果你倾听那些在土地上劳作的人们的意见，你就会做得更好。” 我的。

马吉诺的哲学解释了为什么这些地块已经规划了两年，但长期的实验治疗尚未最终确定。

该研究团队在过去的一年里组建了一个多元化的顾问委员会，并直接征求伊利诺伊州农业学家的意见。 迄今为止，他们已经在全州开展了十多个焦点小组活动，有 140 多名农民参加，而且仍在继续。

Margenot 计划在 2026 年底之前收集农民以及行业和政策利益相关者的反馈。

伊利诺伊州农业局环境政策主任桑杰·索法特 (Sanjay Sofat) 是母校地块顾问委员会成员。 他赞赏玛吉诺从农民那里收集信息的彻底方法。

“农业的现实存在巨大的脱节。 人们发表诸如‘嘿，农民应该做 XYZ’之类的评论，却没有真正理解采用新做法意味着什么，”他说。 “农民必须担心和评估新设备融资、天气限制和许多其他问题的风险。 通过根据农民的意见设计这项实验，我们希望农民能够更放心地采取有利于土地和利润的做法。”

莫罗情节 2.0

母校地块旨在为子孙后代传承历史悠久的莫罗地块的传统。 2026 年，美国庆祝成立 150 周年。 I. 的莫罗地块是世界上第二古老的连续进行的农业实验。

“莫罗地块的全部意义在于解决伊利诺伊州农民所面临的问题，这是我们赠地任务的一部分，”ACES 院长 Germán Bollero 说。 “事情就是这样开始的。 他们邀请农民到校园莫罗地块的延长日。 这对农场社区来说是一件大事。 我们正努力在接下来的 150 年里恢复这一点。”

莫罗地块的当前迭代——一英亩（0.4 公顷），有 24 个子地块——可以容纳在 64 个母校子地块之一内。 留出足够的土地最终扩展到 75 个或更多地块，母校地块代表了更现实的规模，并为研究人员提供了更大的统计能力，以提出自信的管理建议。

新兴主题

虽然研究人员仍在收集农民关于测试内容的意见，但一些主题正在出现，包括水质、土壤健康、再生实践和盈利能力。

母校地块的一个关键特征是其单独监控的排水瓷砖，使研究人员能够实时测试耕作系统、投入和管理实践对水质的影响。 自 2024 年建立这些样地以来，研究人员一直在收集水质基线数据。

“如果我们要耕种该国一些最潮湿的土壤，我们需要瓷砖。 但我们对瓷砖非常被动——我们倾向于把它们放进去然后走开。 农民可以更有意识地管理水资源，”马格诺特说。 “瓷砖排水是否需要权衡？ 绝对地。 让我们优化这些权衡，特别是提高氮肥的使用效率和盈利能力，并最大限度地减少硝酸盐损失。”

马吉诺还希望以帮助农民种植更有利可图的方式设计实验。 例如，他想测试他们是否可以通过种植豆类覆盖作物来避免除草剂和化肥方面的支出。

裘德·霍尔舍 (Jude Holscher) 正在考虑减少除草剂的使用。 霍尔舍尔与父亲一起在克劳福德县务农，他是伊利诺伊州农业局和该县水土保持区的成员。 他了解了玛吉诺特的一个焦点小组后想：“为什么不呢？”

“他们没有就研究地块的用途做出任何决定，所以他们只是从该地区不同农民那里获得见解，”他说。 “我没有隐瞒自己的想法，并表示我希望在美国看到更多再生概念。 我的。 研究情节。”

当霍尔舍尔展望未来时，再生实践对他来说很有意义。

“我的两个儿子分别是 2 岁和 4 岁。 他们确实改变了我对我们正在做的很多事情的看法。 如果你在拖拉机上，他们想和你在一起。 但由于你周围的环境，他们无法乘坐[除草剂]喷雾机。 我告诉我 4 岁的孩子，我希望当他长大到可以使用这台喷雾器时，我们就不再需要使用它了。 这确实是一个长期目标。”

贝丝·巴兰斯基 (Beth Baranski) 自愿担任农民领导的乔戴维斯县土壤和水健康联盟的组织秘书。 她代表她的组织参加了一个焦点小组，该小组的成员对再生实践也越来越感兴趣。

“从传统做法向再生做法的转变带来了挑战，对农民来说可能是一个艰巨的举动。 我们在乔戴维斯县有一批缓慢增长的早期采用者，但相信这些做法可以广泛帮助农民，”她说。 “我们认为，重要的是在地块中体现再生实践，以便研究人员能够理解和比较其好处，以便他们可以与许多依赖大学指导的农民分享这一点。”

未来150年

当研究人员一直在收集信息时，这些地块并没有闲置。 所有这些都被种植在玉米中，用于食品和农业研究基金会为期三年的研究，以测试使用新型高效肥料的拟议方案。

但是，当所有焦点小组的反馈都得到综合并实施第一个长期实验治疗时，马吉诺特说，仍然会有一些方面不是一成不变的。

他说：“我们正在设计处理方法，以便可以修改具体细节，因为莫罗地块已经出现过无数次了，以反映不断变化的农业景观的现实。” “我们不知道 50 年后会出现什么问题，更不用说 150 年后了，但我们正在计划母校地块至少在那么长的时间内继续帮助农民回答这些问题。”

谁是这个故事的幕后黑手？

丽莎·洛克

艺术史学士，物质文化硕士。 前博物馆编辑、护理人员和移植协调员。 自 2021 年起为 Science X 进行编辑。 完整档案 →

罗伯特·伊根

数学生物学学士学位，创意写作硕士学位。 游历广泛，对科学和语言有独特的观点。 完整档案 →
